name: Brunswick Stew
recipeIngredient: 28-ounce can Peeled whole tomatoes; drained and quartered
2 cups Fresh shelled lima beans or 10-ounce package frozen lima beans, thawed and drained
2 cups Corn kernels; fresh or frozen
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into 10 serving pieces: 2 drumsticks, 2 thighs, 2 wings, and 4 breast pieces. Trim as much fat as possible. Rinse and let drain.
2. In a shallow bowl, mix the flour, salt, pepper, thyme, and cayenne.
3. Dredge the damp chicken pieces in the seasoned flour. Reserve any excess seasoned flour for later use.
4. In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over moderately high heat. Add the chicken pieces to the pan in a single layer without crowding them. Cook until golden brown, about 7 minutes per batch. If necessary, lower the heat halfway through cooking to prevent burning.
5. Remove the cooked chicken and transfer it to a large, oven-safe casserole dish.
6. Add the remaining oil to the skillet. Cook the onions over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until soft, about 3 minutes.
7. Add the ham (if using) and cook until lightly browned, about 3 more minutes.
8. Sprinkle the reserved seasoned flour over the ham and onions, cooking for an additional 30-45 seconds while stirring.
9. Pour in half of the chicken stock. Bring to a boil, scraping up any brown bits from the bottom of the pan. Boil briefly until slightly thickened.
10. Pour everything back into the skillet and add the tomatoes, remaining stock, green pepper, and bay leaf. Reduce heat to moderately low and simmer, partially covered, for 15 minutes.
11. Add the lima beans and corn. Simmer uncovered until the chicken and vegetables are tender, about 10-12 more minutes.
12. Skim any fat off the top of the sauce. Remove and discard the bay leaf before serving. Adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper as needed.
